meta.executor: Claude Sonnet (Claude Code agent), headless read-only sweep
meta.method: Enumerated every scenario under .opencode/skills/sk-prompt/manual-testing-playbook/ (root manual-testing-playbook.md = directory/contract listing 4 hub_routing scenarios SP-001..004, plus the standalone compiled-routing/default-mode-compiled-routing.md meta-scenario SP-CR-001 -- 5 scenarios total, matching the packet's own documented Lane C corpus size). For each scenario, ran its exact frontmatter/body prompt text through two commands and diffed the routing decision: (1) COMPILED: `env -u SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING node .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs --hub sk-prompt --prompt "<prompt>"` (default-on cohort, no flag override); (2) LEGACY: `node .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/router-replay.cjs --skill .opencode/skills/sk-prompt --task "<prompt>"`. Compiled targets[].workflowMode was compared against legacy intents as sets, and both were cross-checked against each scenario file's own documented expected_workflow_mode / expected_resources / expected_leaf_resources frontmatter.
meta.repo_mutations: none (git status in cwd shows only the 2 pre-existing, explicitly out-of-scope strays: .opencode/specs/mcp-tooling/008-mcp-aside/001-research/research/research.md, .opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/032-deep-alignment-mode/013-review-remediation/decision-record.md -- plus two untracked concurrent-session archive folders under sk-code/ and sk-doc/ benchmark trees not authored by this sweep -- and this sweep's own new archive folder)

scenarios.prompt: Help me write a better prompt for a customer support chatbot.
scenarios.notes: Generic prompt-improvement request with no named model resolves to routerPolicy.defaultMode=prompt-improve on both engines; legacy resources exactly [prompt-improve/references/depth-framework.md], no over/under-load; compiled==legacy.

scenarios.prompt: What framework and scaffold does DeepSeek-v4-pro want for a coding task?
scenarios.notes: Named small model (DeepSeek-v4-pro) resolves to prompt-models on both engines; legacy cites the model-specific reference (not the generic 7-framework set); compiled==legacy.

scenarios.prompt: Improve this prompt for a small model.
scenarios.notes: Model-adjacent vocabulary ('small model') without a named model id does NOT trip prompt-models on either engine -- routerPolicy.defaultMode wins cleanly, confirming the scenario's success criterion (no silent wrong-mode resolution on vocabulary alone); compiled==legacy.

scenarios.prompt: What prompt-craft profile does GLM-5.2 use for the Z.AI Coding Plan?
scenarios.notes: Second named model (GLM-5.2, distinct from SP-002's DeepSeek) also resolves to prompt-models on both engines, confirming the signal generalizes beyond one example model. Legacy score gap (6 vs 4, delta 2) exceeds AMBIGUITY_DELTA=1 so prompt-improve is correctly NOT kept as a near-tied intent; compiled==legacy.

scenarios.prompt: Help me write a better prompt for a customer support chatbot.
scenarios.notes: Same exact prompt as SP-001 (this scenario's own design: same default-mode fallthrough, scored separately to isolate the serving-authority claim). resolve.cjs returned a full route decision object (hubId/action/selectionKind/targets/effectivePolicyHash/generation) rather than the {servingAuthority:'legacy',hubId} fallback sentinel, which only happens when flagPermitsCompiled(sk-prompt)=true (sk-prompt in DEFAULT_ON_HUBS, flag unset -> 'default' mode) AND the live manifest.json servingAuthority='compiled' (confirmed directly: .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/010-live-activation/activation/sk-prompt/manifest.json reads servingAuthority:'compiled', shadowOnly:false, generation:5) AND compiledRoute() did not throw -- together this proves servingAuthority=compiled was actually exercised, matching the scenario's evidence_serving_authority:compiled and evidence_flag_state:unset frontmatter. evidence_compiled_route:'sk-prompt/prompt-improve' matches targets[0].skillId+'/'+workflowMode exactly. compiled==legacy on workflow-mode and on the resource-contract pair. PROVENANCE NOTE (non-blocking): the scenario frontmatter's evidence_manifest_digest (6f581ac8...) does not match the live manifest.json's whole-file SHA-256 (82381844...), its selectedPolicy.effectivePolicyHash (814cfed8...), nor several other canonicalizations tried (compact/pretty/sorted-keys/selectedPolicy-only JSON hashes) -- same class of harmless digest-provenance drift noted for sk-code's CB-CR-001 (manifest re-serialized without any change to the semantically load-bearing servingAuthority/generation/effectivePolicyHash fields). Not a routing-correctness issue.

summary.lane_c_reference_source: 015-routing-coverage-activation-verification/013-compiled-coverage-buildout/implementation-summary.md lines 71 and 164: 'sk-prompt 5/0' (5 scenarios, 0 drift), independently re-run live during that packet's own 2026-07-21 reconciliation pass and matched exactly.
summary.lane_c_corroboration: 5/5 scenarios PASS, 0/5 compiled-vs-legacy drift in this independent sweep -- directly corroborates the archived Lane C parity result of sk-prompt 5/0 (compiled-serving, 0 drift). Both this sweep's scenario count (5) and drift count (0) match the cited figure exactly.
summary.default_on_hubs_check: DEFAULT_ON_HUBS in .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs still lists exactly 7 hubs (sk-code, system-deep-loop, mcp-tooling, cli-external-orchestration, sk-prompt, sk-design, sk-doc), unchanged by this read-only sweep.
summary.frozen_scorer_sha256_check: All 3 frozen scorer files (load-playbook-scenarios.cjs, router-replay.cjs, score-skill-benchmark.cjs) byte-identical start vs end of this sweep.
